questions.question: What are the six sections that make up the complete Anxiety Toolkit?

options.text: Diagnosis, treatment, recovery, maintenance, prevention, graduation
Understanding anxiety, taming thoughts, calming the storm, the avoidance trap, social connection, and building resilience
Breathing, grounding, surfing, climbing, communicating, and celebrating
Biology, psychology, sociology, philosophy, therapy, and medication

questions.explanation: The six sections are: Understanding Your Anxiety, Taming Your Thoughts (CBT tools), Calming the Storm (distress tolerance), The Avoidance Trap (exposure), Social Connection (communication and boundaries), and Building Resilience (physical foundation and habits).

questions.question: What does the maintenance plan recommend for daily practice?

options.text: At least 1 hour of anxiety exercises
2-5 minutes: practice a resilience habit and check in with your body for tension
Complete a full Thought Record every day
Climb one rung on your Fear Ladder

questions.explanation: The daily maintenance plan (2-5 minutes) includes practicing your chosen resilience habit (mindfulness, gratitude, or self-compassion) and checking in with your body to notice if shoulders, jaw, or stomach are holding tension.

questions.question: Why is it important to identify your personal 'Top 5' tools?

options.text: To rank the tools from best to worst
So you can reach for them quickly when you need them most
To share them on social media
To show your therapist you completed the course

questions.explanation: Knowing your personal top tools makes it easy to reach for them when you need them most. Not every tool resonates equally -- identifying which ones work best for you creates a quick-access personal toolkit.

questions.question: When should you seek professional support according to this lesson?

options.text: Only when anxiety is completely debilitating
When anxiety significantly impacts daily life, you experience unmanageable panic attacks, use substances to cope, or have thoughts of self-harm
Only after completing the course three times
Never -- the course should be sufficient

questions.explanation: Seek professional support if anxiety significantly impacts daily life, work, or relationships; if panic attacks feel unmanageable; if you are using substances to cope; or if you are having thoughts of self-harm. This is not weakness -- it is one of the bravest things you can do.

questions.question: What is the lesson's definition of progress in managing anxiety?

options.text: Never feeling anxious again
Knowing you have the tools and the strength to handle anxiety when it comes
Being able to avoid all stressful situations
Completing every exercise perfectly

questions.explanation: Progress is not about never feeling anxious again. It is about knowing you have the tools and the strength to handle it when it comes. You are not alone, not broken, and far more capable than you know.
